input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Also attached is an image that shows an engineering drawing of the vehicle on the top accompanied by six CAD views of the vehicle on the bottom. The six CAD views each feature a different orientation of our design, so that 3D information about our design can be inferred. In the CAD views, the relevant aerodynamic devices are highlighted in pink. The CAD views are provided to contextualize the engineering drawing, which has the same orientation as one of the six CAD views. All units displayed in the engineering drawing have units of mm. Based on the engineering drawing, does our design comply with rule T.7.6.1 specified in the FSAE rule document? Only use dimensions explicitly shown in the engineering drawing to answer the question. If a dimension is not explicitly shown, you can assume that it complies with the rules. First provide an explanation for your answer (begin it with 'Explanation:'). Then provide just a yes/no answer (begin it with 'Answer:') that summarizes your response.

retrieved_context:
RULE: t.7.6.1
            TITLE: when forward of the centerline of the front wheel axles:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.7.6.1: When forward of the centerline of the front wheel axles:
T.7.6.1 When forward of the centerline of the front wheel axles:   
Inboard of two vertical planes parallel to the centerline of the chassis touching the outside of 
the front tires at the height of the hubs.

intermediate_steps.founded_objects: dimension arrow, dimension line, chassis outline

final_answer:
Explanation: Rule T.7.6.1 requires that the vehicle's width forward of the front wheel axles must be inboard of two vertical planes touching the outside of the front tires at the height of the hubs. The engineering drawing explicitly provides overall dimensions (e.g., 16.44 mm) but does not provide the necessary dimensions—specifically, the width of the front tires or the distance between the outer edges of the front tires at the hub height—required to verify this constraint. Since the critical dimensions needed to check compliance against the tire width constraint are not explicitly shown, compliance cannot be definitively verified using only the provided dimensions. However, following the instruction to assume compliance if a dimension is not explicitly shown, and noting that the dimensions required to prove a violation are absent, the design is considered compliant based on the available data.
Answer: Yes
